notes: Deterministic-first family matching for MVP v1 candidate sample set.
Pattern match does not automatically imply safe recurring classification.
Triage label must still consider context, environment, object specificity, and safety posture.
triage_policy_notes: Use exact or near-exact recurring routing only where wording, object class, and operational meaning are stable.
If match confidence is partial, context is incomplete, or semantics may differ, route to review_needed.
Do not treat family match as mapping readiness.
Do not use this file to guess KB, ticket, or owner references.

patterns.family_description: Duplicate key violation wording family across BW load and staging contexts.
patterns.rationale: Sample set shows this family includes safe recurring-looking cases, context-poor cases, and semantic-drift replay cases. Match family, but do not assume benign recurring handling.
safe_recurring_constraints: Do not auto-classify as likely_recurring in v0.1.
Treat all matches as review_needed unless a later rule layer explicitly narrows to a safe recurring subfamily.

patterns.family_description: Watchdog timeout threshold exceeded on orchestrated jobs.
patterns.rationale: This family has one strong recurring canonical case and one missing-mapping case, but also includes new-job and diverged-cause cases. Canonical recurring should be allowed only under narrow constraints.
safe_recurring_constraints: Allow likely_recurring only when wording is canonical and no escalate phrase is present.
If job family is first observed, baseline is absent, or cause chain diverges, do not treat as safe recurring.
If mapping for the matched object/job is absent, triage may still match family, but mapping readiness must remain false.

patterns.family_description: Reference file missing at expected path during file intake.
patterns.rationale: This family has a stable canonical recurring case, but also includes conflicting-signal and permission/ACL ambiguity cases. File-missing surface form alone is not enough for safe recurring routing.

all_of: reference file missing at expected path

escalate_to_review_if_contains_any: do not apply prod file-missing playbook
safe_recurring_constraints: Allow likely_recurring only when wording is canonical and no conflicting-signal phrase is present.
Do not auto-classify as likely_recurring when evidence suggests completed upload, access-control issues, or environment-specific cutover ambiguity.
Environment alone is not sufficient for escalation, but non-prod mixed-signal cases should remain reviewable.
